packageRules.description: Python interpreter bumps (requires-python) and the matching python-base container image ship together in one PR, separate from regular 3pp bumps. Manual review: bumping the interpreter is rarely a no-op for our 3pp set, so we don't want this automerged alongside boto3 patches.
Pair the python-base Docker image (builder + runner, any registry path) with the interpreter bump above via the shared groupSlug.

Bump the locked version in the lockfile (uv.lock, poetry.lock, Pipfile.lock, compiled requirements.txt) even when the declared range in pyproject.toml still satisfies the new version. Without this, ranges like `cachetools >=5,<8` never produce a PR for an in-range release (e.g. 7.1.2 -> 7.1.4), and the lockfile drifts. Scoped to lockfile-aware managers only: pip_requirements and pip_setup are intentionally excluded because they typically have no separate lockfile, and update-lockfile would skip those deps entirely.
